bluetooth_confirm.title: Difluid Microbalance
bluetooth_confirm.description:
Do you want to add **{name}** to Home Assistant?

Leave the fields blank for older (cleartext) scales. If your scale uses encrypted firmware (sensors stay at 0 and the log shows `DA DA` packets), enter your DiFluid SDK license key. The model is optional and defaults to the standard Microbalance model.

r2_license.title: Difluid R2 Extract — License
r2_license.description: A **{name}** was found. Enter your DiFluid SDK license key to enable communication. You can get a license key from [digitizefluid.com](https://digitizefluid.com).

user.title: Difluid Device
user.description:
Select your Difluid device. If nothing appears in the list, make sure the device is on and Bluetooth is working in Home Assistant. You can also enter the MAC address manually (format: `AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF`) — find it in the official DiFluid app or via **nRF Connect** on your phone.

The License Key is only needed for encrypted-firmware scales (sensors stay at 0 and the log shows `DA DA` packets). Leave it blank otherwise. Model is optional.

error.no_devices_found: No Difluid devices were found nearby. Make sure the device is on and in Bluetooth range.
error.invalid_license: License key is too short. Please enter a valid DiFluid SDK license key.
